title: Computer Use Latency
concept: Managing the timing gap between triggering a click and waiting for the window manager to render the update.

exercises.prompt: What do keyboard shortcuts help you do when using a computer?

options.text: Speed up your actions while using software
Change the color of the screen

exercises.explain: Keyboard shortcuts help speed up your actions while using software, making it easier to navigate and perform tasks efficiently.

cards.title: What is Latency?
cards.body: Latency is the delay between your action, like clicking a button, and the computer showing the result.

cards.title: Understanding Latency
cards.body: Computer latency is the time it takes for your clicks or commands to be recognized and for the computer to respond.

cards.title: Latency Like Waiting for a Friend
cards.body: Think of latency like waiting for a friend to respond to a message. You send them a text, and you wait for them to reply.

cards.title: Example of Latency
cards.body: When you click to open a new webpage, there’s a short wait before it appears. That wait is called latency.

cards.title: Myth: No Delay in Clicking
cards.body: Some believe once they click, things happen instantly. Latency means there will always be a slight delay.

cards.title: Latency Comparison
cards.body: Understanding latency helps you manage your actions better.

right.text: Wait for the update to appear

exercises.prompt: Why is it important to understand computer latency?

options.text: To manage your expectations and actions better
To reduce the number of clicks you make
To isolate your computer from the internet

exercises.explain: Understanding computer latency helps you manage your expectations and actions better, so you know it might take a moment for results to show.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Latency is the time it takes for a computer to respond after an action.
exercises.explain: True! Latency is indeed the time that passes between your action and the computer's response.

exercises.prompt: Latency is the gap between triggering a click and waiting for the ________ to render the update.
exercises.sentence: Latency is the gap between triggering a click and waiting for the window manager to render the update.
exercises.explain: The correct word is 'window manager', as it relates directly to the timing of the visual update after a click.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence was generated by AI?

options.text: After clicking, you might wait a moment to see changes on your screen.
Latencies are puppies and they are slow at running.
Latency means your computer is broken when it takes a long time.

exercises.explain: The AI-generated one is number two about puppies, which doesn't make sense in this context, while the other two are about computer latency.

exercises.prompt: What should you do if you notice high latency when clicking on a program?

options.text: Close the program and try again later.
Wait a few seconds and then try again to give it time to respond.
Click as fast as you can to skip the waiting time.
Shut down your computer and restart.

exercises.explain: You should wait a few seconds and then try again to give it time to respond. Understanding that latency is a natural part of how computers process tasks helps you manage your actions.
